title: Eye Tips
shortDescription: Reduce strain and protect your eyes during long hours in front of a screen

tips.title: The 20-20-20 Rule
tips.body: Every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for at least 20 seconds to relax your focusing muscles and reduce digital eye strain.

tips.title: Blink More Often
tips.body: Screen work often reduces blinking. Conscious blinking helps keep your eyes moist and lowers irritation.

tips.title: Position Your Screen Well
tips.body: Keep the top of your monitor at or slightly below eye level to avoid extra neck strain and uncomfortable gaze angles.

title: Posture Tips
shortDescription: Keep your body aligned and comfortable throughout the workday

tips.title: Keep Feet Grounded
tips.body: Rest both feet flat on the floor or on a footrest to support your lower back and reduce tension through your hips.

tips.title: Keep the Screen at Arm's Length
tips.body: A comfortable viewing distance helps you avoid leaning forward and keeps your shoulders from rounding.

tips.title: Reset Your Posture Often
tips.body: A quick posture reset every hour is more realistic and effective than trying to stay perfectly upright all day.

title: Stress Tips
shortDescription: Calm your mind, manage daily pressure, and improve focus

tips.title: Name the Stressor
tips.body: When you feel overwhelmed, briefly identify the exact source of pressure. Clarity makes stress feel more manageable.

tips.title: Use a Short Breathing Reset
tips.body: A one- to three-minute breathing session can lower tension quickly and help you return to your next task with more control.

tips.title: Break the Task Down
tips.body: If stress comes from workload, split the task into one immediate next step instead of trying to solve everything at once.

title: Focus & Fatigue
shortDescription: Reignite concentration and maintain steady energy throughout the day

tips.title: Single-Task for a Short Window
tips.body: A focused 20-minute block often produces better work than trying to multitask across several tabs and conversations.

tips.title: Stand Up Before the Slump
tips.body: A quick standing or stretching break before fatigue peaks helps restore alertness faster than pushing through.

tips.title: Protect Your Attention
tips.body: Silence nonessential notifications during deep work so your brain is not constantly forced to reorient.

title: Hydration & Nutrition
shortDescription: Fuel your body with hydration and simple, energizing meals

tips.title: Keep Water Visible
tips.body: A water bottle in sight acts as a reminder and makes it easier to stay hydrated throughout the day.

tips.title: Choose Steady Energy Snacks
tips.body: Pairing protein or fiber with carbs can help you avoid the sharp crashes that come from sugary snacks alone.

tips.title: Keep Lunch Balanced
tips.body: A balanced lunch supports concentration better than a heavy meal that leaves you sleepy in the afternoon.

title: Sleep & Recovery
shortDescription: Rest deeply to recharge your mind and body

tips.title: Keep a Stable Sleep Schedule
tips.body: Going to bed and waking up at similar times helps your body recover more consistently.

tips.title: Reduce Bright Light at Night
tips.body: Lowering screen brightness and harsh light exposure in the evening can make it easier to wind down.

tips.title: Create a Work Shutdown Ritual
tips.body: A clear transition at the end of the workday makes it easier for your brain to stop replaying unfinished tasks.

title: Mental Health
shortDescription: Build emotional balance and resilience against daily stress

tips.title: Check In With Yourself
tips.body: A quick emotional check-in helps you notice when tension is building before it becomes burnout.

tips.title: Talk Before It Piles Up
tips.body: If work pressure keeps growing, discussing it early with a teammate, manager, or trusted person is often more effective than waiting.

tips.title: Set Clear Boundaries
tips.body: Protecting focus time and rest time supports long-term emotional resilience.

title: Mobility & Stretching
shortDescription: Stay flexible and prevent stiffness caused by prolonged sitting

tips.title: Stretch Briefly but Often
tips.body: Short movement breaks spread across the day are easier to keep and more effective than a single long break.

tips.title: Move Through Full Range
tips.body: Gentle full-range movement helps restore mobility better than tiny, rushed motions.

tips.title: Pair Motion With Breathing
tips.body: Slow breathing during stretches helps your body relax and makes the movement feel smoother.

title: Productivity & Time Balance
shortDescription: Work smarter, not longer — balance focus and rest

tips.title: Define the Next Action
tips.body: When you know the next concrete step, it is easier to restart work after an interruption or short break.

tips.title: Build Breaks Into the Day
tips.body: Deliberate pauses help preserve output across the full day instead of burning energy early.

tips.title: Time-Box Shallow Work
tips.body: Email, chat, and admin tasks expand easily, so giving them a fixed window helps protect deeper work.

title: Breathing & Relaxation
shortDescription: Regain calm and focus through intentional breathing

tips.title: Lengthen the Exhale
tips.body: A slightly longer exhale often helps your nervous system settle more quickly than shallow breathing.

tips.title: Drop Your Shoulders
tips.body: Relaxing your jaw and shoulders while breathing helps the entire exercise feel more effective.

tips.title: Use Micro Sessions
tips.body: Even one minute of intentional breathing can help reset your focus between demanding tasks.
